publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-10-28 18:46:48 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: The materials by and relating to Leary in this collection were assembled by rare books and contemporary art dealer John McWhinnie.

This collection contains circa 150 items comprised of manuscripts and documents by Leary; letters to and from Leary, including additional correspondence concerning him; documents and manuscripts relating to Leary's colleagues and associates in the psychedelic movement, and the various institutions and organizations they created; and psychedelic-related ephemera.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Jude Corina & Lin Sen Chai, December 2017

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Timothy Leary (1920-1996) was an American psychologist and writer known for advocating the exploration of the therapeutic potential of psychedelic drugs.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The materials by and relating to Leary in this collection were assembled by rare books and contemporary art dealer John McWhinnie.

This collection contains circa 150 items comprised of manuscripts and documents by Leary; letters to and from Leary, including additional correspondence concerning him; documents and manuscripts relating to Leary's colleagues and associates in the psychedelic movement, and the various institutions and organizations they created; and psychedelic-related ephemera.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Timothy Leary Manuscripts and Ephemera, #8593. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I: Leary manuscripts and documents
Typescript photocopy, Declaration of Independence for Dr. Timothy Leary. July 4, 1971 (Draft)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement defending Leary, with autograph note: "Allen Ginsberg arranged! / [sketch of smiling heart] / "Timothy Leary / 9-19-95." Draft text for pamphlet of the same title.

did.unittitle: Declaration of Independence for Dr. Timothy Leary. July 4, 1971

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprint from Journal of Personality. Vol. 20, no. 2. December, 1951. Pages 143-161

did.unittitle: Psychedelic Prayers after the tao te ching

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New Hyde Park, N.Y.: University Books. n.d.

did.unittitle: Summary of the Dissertation Submitted in Partial Satisfaction of the Requirements for the Degree of Doctor of Philosophy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: University of California, Graduate Division, Northern Section.

did.unittitle: The Neo-American Church Catechism and Handbook: A Review by Timothy Leary
A Constructive Proposal to End the LSD Crisis
Religious Implications of Consciousness Expanding Substances

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprinted from Religious Education

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description of personal DMT experience

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summary of LSD research at Harvard for a Los Angles audience

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An addendum describing Leary's recent activities since the publication "of this book" (presumably Starseed).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Check for five dollars payable to "NAACP," on American Trust Company account, Oakland, CA. Cancellation stamps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Leary's essay, "Progress Toward Solving the Problem of Race Relations," (pp. 41-43).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A typed description by Leary, heavily annotated, of his first meeting with Huxley, his initial purchase of LSD, and the group they gathered to conduct the first experiment (which included Huxley)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leary's description of taking LSD in Mexico and engaging in a threesome

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description and purposes of the League for Spiritual Discovery, "a non-profit religious association" which advocated the use of LSD. Leary listed as first member of "Board of Guides" (p.5).

did.unittitle: United States Corps of Cadets Card, issued to Leary
The Religious Experience: Its Production and Interpretation
Typescript of "revision of the article on drugs," for Esquire magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With attached carbon of March 12, 1964 letter (unsigned) from Leary to Harold Hayes, editor of Esquire, resubmitting the manuscript; typewritten corrections throughout

did.unittitle: Reactions To Psilocybin Administered in a Supportive Environment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprint from The Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease, Vol. 137, No. 6, December 1963

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leary justifies his plea of not guilty to federal drug charges.

did.unittitle: Americans and Mushrooms in a Naturalistic Environment: A Preliminary Report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three original typescript pages from a longer interview of Leary, which has been heavily edited and emended in autograph, possibly by him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original typescript

did.unittitle: "One night in the winter I crossed the Rockies…"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original typescript story featuring Little Richard, Fatty Arbuckle, Floyd Patterson, and talking goats.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original typescript of article describing a San Francisco peace rally.

did.unittitle: A New Behavior Change Program Using Psilocybin
Typescript essay, untitled. ("A journalist in the Haight-Ashbury is hung on a strange dilemma.")

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annotated in Leary's hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph notes in pencil and ink, on verso of circular letter, unsigned, from James Goddard, Commissioner of Food and Drugs

did.unittitle: A group of photocopied legal contracts between Leary and Michel- Gustave Hauchard relating to the publishing and film rights of various Leary works

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many with original ink annotations in Leary's hand

did.unittitle: Series II: Letters to and from Leary (and among his circle)
Photocopies of Correspondence with Allen Ginsburg
Photocopies of Correspondence with Aldous Huxley
Autograph Letter Signed to Michael Hollengshead

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report on his happiness and optimism about his legal situation: "We too are enjoying Heaven…"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With: Photocopy of "United States of America vs. Timothy Leary: Change of Counsel."

did.unittitle: Autograph Letter Signed "T' to Dr. Gene Schoenfeld

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leary thanks him for sending various publications and speculates about possible titles for his forthcoming book.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter signed, "James Peter Cavagnaro" to "Jan."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writing from prison, he praises Leary's and Hesse's ideas on liberation and spiritual energy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Together with: Appeal Against Further Trial & Persecution of Dr. Timothy Leary

did.unittitle: Typed letter signed, "Arthur Kleps" to "Mr. Volkening."
Typed letter signed, "Paul" to "Tim or whoever."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Krassner inquiring if Leary will be conducting a seminar at Millbrook in late November

did.unittitle: Typed letter signed, "Paul" to "Tim, Dick, and Ralph."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Krassner informing Leary that his new article was to be entitled "Dr. Leary – or How I Learned to Transcend Ego and Expand My Consciousness (Notes from a Found Weekend)."

did.unittitle: Typed note signed, "A.C. Spectorsky" to "Dr. Leary."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stapled to: Typed letter signed, from "Tim Leary" to "Mr.Spectorsky." With: Playboy Interview: Timothy Leary. Also with: Leary, Timothy. Typescript on Ira Cohen, LSD, and Cannabis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Watts states that the both the FDA and Myron Stolaroff of the National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH) suspected the current liquid LSD in circulation had very serious impurities

did.unittitle: Typed letter signed in type, "Your Flower Peter" to "Dr. Timothy Leary."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Orlovsky — Ginsberg's lover — writing Leary about his experiences with LSD.

did.unittitle: Typed letters signed, "Art Kleps" to "Dr. Kissinger" and "Editor, Time Magazine."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kleps writes Kissinger in the midst of the Watergate crisis, and states the good that might come if the conspirators ended up in the same prison as Leary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A draft of Leary's "Signal From Galactic Transmission Center to Planet Earth," which presents as "call home" from extraterrestrial species to the human race.

did.unittitle: Typed letter signed, from "Richard Alpert" to "Dr. Hofmann"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A letter written by Alpert on behalf of Leary, who was in Mexico at the time.

did.unittitle: Typed letter signed, to Dr. Humphry Osmond, with partial draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Metzner disputes Osmond's critical positionson both the Psychedelic Review and Leary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describing his travel plans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Metzger encloses (not present) several brochures about upcoming psychedelic seminars and workshops.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On verso: Family Dog Presents Poster.

did.unittitle: Large brown mailing envelope, annotated and signed by Timothy Leary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A typewr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Announcement of League for Spiritual Discovery meetings.

did.unittitle: The LSD Spiritual Revolution and Technological Change

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sent as mailer; verso of second leaf franked and addressed in autograph from "B Ehrlich" in Van Nuys, CA

did.unittitle: Come and Share in the Magic of Millbrook Weekend Live-Ins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bibliography: Recent Publications About Theories and Research on Psychedelic Experiences.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With Photocopy of drawing of word sections; "Me" in the center

did.unittitle: Application for Membership to International Federation for Internal Freedom (IFIF)
Program for the Re-Affirmation of the Eternal Union of Carol Mann and Bob Ross

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With: Printed card, naming Leary as "presiding p

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Invitation to a benefit for the Timothy Leary Defense Fund

did.unittitle: Neo-American Church Catechism and Handbook Brochure

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Together with: Card insert: "How to Use Your Head." Mass mailer for presentation by Leary and Alpert on Sunday, November 29, 1964, at Town Hall in New York. Leary has amended the brochure to eliminate both the Castalia Foundation and Richard Alpert's names throughout, and change the title to "Psychedelic Workshops Conducted by Timothy Leary, Ph.D. and Ralph Metzner, Ph.D."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Invitation to a public baseball tournament, as well as other psychedelic news.

did.unittitle: Selected Bibliography on the Safety of LSD-25

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A small selection of books and articles supporting the safety of LSD, some with additional quotes and reviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An announcement for a lecture by Leary and Richard Alpert.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program for a League event, with Leary acting as "Guide," Ralph Metzner as "Harry, the Imprisoned Intellectual," and others in other roles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Psychedelic Review with TLS, from Felix Morrow and Ralph Metzger as well as a Psychedelic Review Subscription Direct Mail Package.

did.unittitle: International Federation for Internal Freedom Membership Package

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including; Statement on LSD and Psilocybin; Application for Membership; I.F.I.F. Newsletters; Marketing Letter, Typed, to "Dear Friend" from Leary; Present Program: IFIF – Cambridge; New Statement of Purposes; Sample Letter Draft; Paradise Lost by Mexico LSD Colony; Page 3 of Mimeograph Letter; Note.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A marketing brochure for a series of workshops led by Leary and Metzger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A marketing brochure for a series of weekend workshops led by Leary, Metzner, and Alpert at the Castalia Foundation in Millbrook.

did.unittitle: On "Alternative Realities," "Biverses," "Multiverses" and Occultism in General

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An excerpt from Kleps's longer text, Millbrook.

did.unittitle: An Introduction to the Field of Consciousness Expansion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With an Outline of the Effects and Usage of the Psychedelic Drugs

did.unittitle: Chromosomal Abnormalities in Leukocytes From LSD-25 Users
The Avatar of You is You
Castalia Foundation Millbrook Summer School Psychedelic Training Courses Brochure
Proposal for research on consciousness-altering drugs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A request for an $11,000 grant to study the effects of psilocybin and mescaline at Harvard, with Leary and Alpert among the "consultants."

did.unittitle: The Three Who Made a Revolution

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A study of the work of Leary, Alpert and Metzner

did.unittitle: Points of Distinction Between Sedative and Hallucinogenic Drugs
Bay Area Writers Appeal to Swiss Government for Leary Political Asylum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement Signed by Kesey, Ferlinghetti and Others, Calls Leary Fellow Author Without Country

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Opening line: "The plan of this session was to have a solo experience."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Psychedelic Review #10.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On Millbrook and LSD

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Begins: "Art if not life, what? Life if not art, why?..."

did.unittitle: Draft of The LSD Issue: A New Look at the World
Manuscript Notes for The LSD Issue: A New Look at the World
The LSD Issue: A New Look at the World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A typescript teleplay for video featuring Dana Farnsworth, Dr. Theodore Rothman, Dr. Roy Grinker, and various experimental subjects, among others. No record of it having been produced has been found. Hitchcock-Balding Productions, to which the teleplay is addressed, also produced several of Leary's live speaking events, and had been engaged by the League of Spiritual Discovery to handle their public business.

did.unittitle: Timothy Leary Defense Fund Declaration of Principle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leary Defense Fund

did.unittitle: Committee for the Reform of Marihuana Laws Statement of Purpose

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leary Defense Fund

did.unittitle: Acid Beat presents the Psychedelic Glossary
Psychedelic Peace Fellowship Affirmation of Purpose and Membership Application

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Listing a lecture by Leary on "Psychedelics and Religion."

did.unittitle: A Gathering of the Tribes for a Human Be-In Poster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of performers includes Ginsberg and Leary.

did.unittitle: Printed program for The LSD Conference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Greater Freedom for the Socio-Economic Bind Through Fasting; Psycles Research; A Safe and Successful Program For the Solving of Human Problems and the transformation of Lives

did.unittitle: First Meeting (organizational) of the tentative Society for Psychedelic Research
Free University of Berkeley Spring 1969 Catalogue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First course listed, on cover, is "Psychedelic Psychology," taught by Leary.

did.unittitle: LSD and the Psychedelic Movement. Printed lecture program

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Oregon State System of Higher Education

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Listing a schedule of lectures in Portland, Oregon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issued by the Detroit, Michigan group, LEMAR, calling for the legalization of marijuana.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet for "psychedelic" classes in fall 1968.

did.unittitle: Can Drugs Lead You to God?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally published in Fate Magazine in May, 1963.

did.unittitle: Physics as a Mandala for Human Consciousness

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two Drafts

did.unittitle: Notes on the Effect of the "Magic Mushroom" on my own Person

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Details observations from experiment on a 25-year-old poet on psilocybin.
